sourceMovie.title: Rushmore
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Rushmore for the same reason it broke through in 1998: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Wes Anderson keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: 10 Things I Hate About You
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Rushmore clicked for you, 10 Things I Hate About You (1999) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Rushmore?
faq.answer: Try 10 Things I Hate About You, Dazed and Confused, The Half of It—each shares DNA with Rushmore while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Rushmore?
faq.answer: Rushmore (1998) is best described as Comedy, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Rushmore worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
